ref: and the Lord sent burning sulfur down like rain on Sodom and Gomorrah.
text: Na AWURADE tɔɔ sɔfe ne ogya firi ɔsoro guu Sodom ne Gomora so.
translated: The Lord rained down fire and brimstone from heaven upon Sodom and Gomorrah.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: Indeed, God created man in His own image. And in the image of God He created him, male and female He created them.

ref: Later, Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan, and when Dedan grew up, he had three sons: Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
text: Na Yoksan woo Seba ne Dedan. Na Dedan mma ne Asurfoɔ, Letusfoɔ ne Leumfoɔ.
translated: I am Yoksan, son of Seba and Dedan. Dedan also gave birth to Asurfoɔ, Letusfoɔ and Leumfoɔ.

ref: In a dream he saw a ladder that reached from earth to heaven, and God's angels were going up and down on it.
text: Na ɔsoo daeɛ, na hwɛ, na atwedeɛ bi si fam a atifi pem soro, na hwɛ, Nyankopɔn abɔfoɔ redi so aforosiane.
translated: When you see a beautiful thing, look at it, and when you see a strange thing, look at it, for the children of God are many.

ref: “God understands what will happen on the day you eat fruit from that tree. You will see what you have done, and you will know the difference between right and wrong, just as God does.”
text: na Onyankopɔn nim sɛ da a mobɛdi bie no, mo ani bɛbue, na moayɛ sɛ Onyankopɔn ahunu papa ne bɔne.
translated: He knows that on the day you will die, your body will become weak, and you will realize that God is truly great and powerful.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: Indeed, God created man in His image. And in the image of God He created him, male and female He created them.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: If you go to any food, it becomes your food and theirs.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said to Abraham, "As for Sarai your wife, do not call her Sarai anymore, but call her Sarah."

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Because of my mother and my children, I will serve you. You yourself know the service I render to you.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: Jacob also called the place Peniel, because he said: I have seen God face to face, and yet my life is preserved.

ref: And I have given the green plants as food for everything else that breathes, including animals, both wild and tame, and birds. And so it was.
text: Na asase so mmoadoma nyinaa ne ewiem nnomaa nyinaa ne mmoa a wɔwea asase soɔ nyinaa, mede nhahan mono mama wɔn sɛ wɔnni. Na ɛyɛɛ saa.
translated: All the creatures on earth, all the plants and all the things that were created on earth, I have seen them all and I know them.

ref: The man answered, “I was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!”
text: Na ɔse: Metee wo nka wɔ turom hɔ no, mesuroe, ɛfiri sɛ meda adagya, na mehuntae.
translated: I am sorry: I can't talk here, I am busy, because I am working, and I am tired.

ref: and in three days the king will cut off your head. He will hang your body on a pole, and birds will come and peck at it.
text: na aka nnansa na Farao ayi wo afiri ha, na wakɔsɛne wo dua so ma nnomaa adi wo nam afiri wo ho.
translated: When the time came for Pharaoh to die, he was buried in his tomb, and his servants placed his treasures with him in his tomb.

ref: The next morning, when Joseph went to see the men, he could tell they were upset,
text: Na Yosef kɔɔ wɔn nkyɛn anɔpa kɔhwɛɛ wɔn, na hwɛ, na wɔayeyɛ mosomosoo.
translated: And Joseph saw them from afar, and behold, they were bowing down to the ground.

ref: So Isaac named the well Shibah, and the town is still called Beersheba. Esau's Foreign Wives
text: Na ɔfrɛɛ no Siba. Enti kuro no din de Beer-seba de bɛsi nnɛ yi ara. Esau ananafoɔ yerenom
translated: This is why they called the city Beer-sheba, because he swore there.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Na Onyankopɔn bɔɔ onipa wɔ ne suban so. Aane, Nyankopɔn suban so na ɔbɔɔ no, ɔbarima ne ɔbaa na ɔbɔɔ wɔn.
translated: God created man in his own image. And God created them, male and female.

ref: Store up enough food both for yourself and for them.
text: Na wo deɛ, boaboa aduane nyinaa bi ano kɔ mu, na ayɛ wo ne wɔn aduane.
translated: Whatever you do, don't go into any food, and it will be your food and theirs.

ref: Abraham, from now on your wife's name will be Sarah instead of Sarai.
text: Na Onyankopɔn ka kyerɛɛ Abraham sɛ: Wo yere Sarai deɛ, mfrɛ no Sarai bio, na mmom Sara.
translated: And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, henceforth she shall be called Sarah.

ref: You know how hard I've worked for you, so let me take my wives and children and leave.”
text: Fa me yerenom ne me mma a wɔn nti mesomm woɔ no ma me. Wo ara wonim me som a masom woɔ.
translated: Because of my mother and my children, I have to endure you. You yourself know the pain I'm going through because of you.

ref: Jacob said, “I have seen God face to face, and I am still alive.” So he named the place Peniel.
text: Na Yakob too ɛhɔ din Peni-el, ɛfiri sɛ ɔse: Me ne Onyankopɔn adi nhunuanimu, nso me kra anya nkwa.
translated: Jacob also called the place Peniel, saying, “For I have seen God face to face, and yet my life was spared.”
